---
title: "Developer Guide"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Developer Guide}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

## Adding New Endpoints

To add a new endpoint to the API:

### 1. Create an endpoint helper function

Create a new file in `R/` following the naming pattern `add_*_endpoint.R`:

```r
# R/add_myendpoint_endpoint.R

#' Add my endpoint to plumber router
#'
#' @param pr A plumber router object
#' @return The modified plumber router
#' @keywords internal
add_myendpoint_endpoint <- function(pr) {
  pr$handle(
    "GET",
    "/myendpoint",
    function(param1, param2) {
      # Your endpoint logic here
      codeminer::my_function(param1, param2)
    }
  )
  pr
}
```

### 2. Register in `create_codeminer_api()`

Add your endpoint to the router composition in `R/create_codeminer_api.R`:

```r
create_codeminer_api <- function() {
  pr <- plumber::pr()
  
  pr <- add_description_endpoint(pr)
  pr <- add_codes_endpoint(pr)
  pr <- add_myendpoint_endpoint(pr)  # Add your endpoint here
  
  pr
}
```

### 3. Write tests

Create tests in `tests/testthat/test-add_myendpoint_endpoint.R`:

```r
test_that("add_myendpoint_endpoint adds /myendpoint path", {
  test_endpoint_addition(
    add_myendpoint_endpoint,
    "myendpoint",
    "/myendpoint"
  )
})
```

The `test_endpoint_addition()` helper (defined in `tests/testthat/helper-endpoint-tests.R`) verifies that:

- The endpoint function exists and is callable
- The specified path is added to the router
- The endpoint appears in the API paths list
